title: Question: BLKANDNBLK
body:
---


Author Name: **Todd Strader** (@toddstrader)
Original Redmine Message: 1763 from https://www.veripool.org



---

I'm reading this warning under the BLKANDNBLK description in the manual:
```
Ignoring this warning may make Verilator simulations differ from other simulators.

```
Can anyone tell me when this scenario would arise?  From what I can tell through experimentation this is safe:
```
logic [1:0] foo;
assign foo[0] = 1'b0;
always @(posedge clk) foo[1] <= bar;

```
while this is not:
```
logic [1:0] foo;
assign foo[1] = 1'b0;  // both continuous and blocking assignments set this bit
always @(posedge clk) foo[1] <= bar;

```
Is the only danger here when the two assignments collide on particular bits of the LHS?  And if Verilator were made to detect these collisions, could this be demoted from scary, bad thing to bad style?
